differentials.name: Large left atrium: mitral stenosis + LLL atelectasis
differentials.raw: Large left atrium: mitral stenosis + LLL atelectasis

differentials.name: Foreign body: food aspiration, endotracheal intubation
differentials.raw: Foreign body: food aspiration, endotracheal intubation

differentials.name: airless collapse within minutes to hours
differentials.raw: airless collapse within minutes to hours

differentials.name: collapsed lung NOT completely airless
differentials.raw: collapsed lung NOT completely airless (up to 40% residual air)

context: Signs: √  local increase in lung density √  crowding of pulmonary vessels √  bronchial rearrangement √  displacement of fissures √  displacement of hilum √  mediastinal shift √  elevation of hemidiaphragm √  cardiac rotation √  approximation of ribs √  compensatory overinflation of normal lung Obstructive Atelectasis RESORPTIVE ATELECTASIS Pathophysiology: sum of partial gas pressures in venous blood perfusing atelectatic region is less than atmospheric pressure, → gradual resorption of air trapped distal to site of obstruction; continuing secretion into small airways leads to consolidation →
